Nepal Objects to Kailash Mansarovar Route via Lipulekh

Nepal has objected to India–China plans to conduct the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra via Lipulekh Pass, falsely claiming the route passes through its territory.

Kathmandu reiterated that Lipulekh, Kalapani, and Limpiyadhura belong to Nepal, citing historical treaties, and said it was not consulted on the decision.

Indian Ministry of External Affairs rejected the claim, stating the route has been in use since 1954 and termed Nepal’s position “untenable”, while remaining open to dialogue.

A dramatic overnight rescue unfolded in Uttar Pradesh’s Gorakhpur district as two children were left stranded atop a water tank after the access ladder was damaged. Responding swiftly to the state administration’s request, the Indian Air Force deployed its Mi-17 V5 helicopter under Central Air Command. In a high-risk early morning operation around 5:30 AM, both children were successfully rescued and brought to safety.

This mission once again highlights the IAF’s unmatched professionalism, rapid response capability, and commitment to saving civilian lives under challenging conditions. The Mi-17 V5, known as the backbone of IAF’s helicopter fleet, proved its versatility yet again in a critical humanitarian mission.
